svg.title: urukube — Repository Calling Diagram
svg.desc: Eleven urukube repos across four layers: orchestrator calls orchestrator-plane-setup via workflow_call; orchestrator-plane-setup runs six sequential Terraform stages each calling a leaf module; release-workflow is shared by every repo.
svg.text: Eleven repos · six sequential Terraform stages · one shared release workflow
calling repo — no Terraform code · only .tfvars + workflow triggers
S3 state bucket: urukube-orchestrator-tfstate · tfvars: orchestrator.tfvars
Checked out at runtime · runs 6 sequential Terraform plan-approve-apply stages · reads tfvars + S3 bucket from caller inputs
Wires ESO to AWS Secrets Manager
Must run AFTER ④ (ESO CRDs needed)
Entry-point calling repo (no TF code — only .tfvars + workflow triggers)
Reusable workflow repo (checked out at runtime; owns all Terraform logic in subdirectories)
Leaf Terraform module repos (pinned via git source ref=vX.Y.Z; consumed by orchestrator-plane-setup only)
Terraform module source (git clone at plan time)
S3 remote state read (data.terraform_remote_state — stages ③–⑥ all read from ② eks-infra)
① orchestrator-secret-mgmt → ② eks-infra → ③ eks-essential-addons → ④ orchestrator-custom-addons → ⑤ eso-configuration → ⑥ argocd-configuration
⑥ argocd-configuration → ⑤ eso-configuration → ④ orchestrator-custom-addons → ③ eks-essential-addons → ② eks-infra → ① orchestrator-secret-mgmt
